Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF BARRY TARANTO'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted. No opposition filed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Oct-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MATTHEW D. CARLSON, ESQ. of Carlson Legal Services, Tel No. 415-817-1470 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. SHANNON LISS-RIORDAN of Lichten & Liss-Riordan, P.C. in Boston, Massachusetts also present;
Atty. THOMAS M. MCINERNY, ESQ. of Ogletree, Deakins, Nash, Smoak & Stewart, P.C., Tel No. 415-442-4871 appearing for Defendant Washio, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF BARRY TARANTO'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE, the applications for pro hac vice admission of Shannon Liss-Riordan and Adelaide Pagano are denied. Defendants have presented ample evidence establishing that Ms. Liss-Riordan, Ms. Pagano and the firm of Lichten & Liss-Riordan are regularly engaged in substantial legal activities in California and thus are not eligible for pro hac vice admission. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
